title: aspidium filix-mas var. marginale
intro: Aspidium filix-mas var. marginale is a rare and lesser-known variety of the common fern, Aspidium filix-mas, native to Europe and Asia. It is also known as the margined lady fern or the hairy fern. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.
